message:
fix(fcp-sdk): properly escape JSON Pointer path segments per RFC 6901

JSON Pointer (RFC 6901) requires special characters to be escaped in path
segments: '~' must become '~0' and '/' must become '~1'. The format_path
function was emitting raw key strings, causing path resolution failures
when keys contained these characters.

Changes:
- Add escape_json_pointer helper function implementing RFC 6901 encoding
- Apply escaping to PathSegment::Key values in format_path
- Order of replacement matters: ~ before / (standard mandates ~0 then ~1)

Example impact:
- Key "foo/bar" now correctly formats as "/foo~1bar" instead of "/foo/bar"
- Key "~config" now correctly formats as "/~0config" instead of "/~config"

This is essential for FCP operations that navigate JSON structures with
keys containing special characters, such as file paths or URL fragments.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
